Neural Network Basics: A neural network is composed of layers of interconnected nodes (neurons) that transform inputs through learned weight matrices and nonlinear activation functions. The forward pass computes predictions, while backpropagation computes gradients of the loss with respect to all weights. Gradient descent and its variants (SGD, Adam, RMSProp) iteratively update weights to minimize the training loss. Understanding vanishing and exploding gradients is critical when designing deep architectures.
